key_insight: Optimizing thermal spray parameters, especially with HVOF, can significantly improve coating density, hardness, adhesion, and wear resistance.
engineering_relevance: These coatings can extend the service life of critical industrial components by improving protection against abrasive and erosive wear.
method: Deposited ceramic, metallic, and composite powders using thermal spray methods such as plasma spraying and HVOF.
Compared coating microstructure, adhesion strength, and wear resistance under different operating conditions.
Evaluated how process parameters affect coating performance across deposition techniques.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experimental evaluation showing that optimized spray settings improve coating density, hardness, and wear resistance, with HVOF-sprayed composite coatings performing best.
watch_item: Results are comparative and parameter-dependent, so performance may not transfer directly to other materials, substrates, or operating environments.
paper_title: Design and Fabrication of Wear-Resistant Coatings Using Thermal Spray Techniques
